title: F-SLOT-PICKER-LIVE-PROBE: live-API health gate for claude_slot_pick.hexa (cycle 2 / fan-out 1/5)
scope: Add a live-API health gate to ~/core/hive/tool/claude_slot_pick.hexa so that --pick refuses to return profiles whose OAuth refreshToken has been revoked Anthropic-side (currently 11/12 of the pool). The gate round-trips `claude -p ping --model claude-haiku-4-5` per candidate (5s timeout, 30s cache) and walks LRU order until a healthy candidate is found, or aborts with raw 66 trailer when all 12 are unhealthy.

background_b1_residue.observed_state: B.1 left the 12-profile pool with 11 revoked refreshTokens. _pick_lru's gate (file existence + cooldown windows) considered them all available; dispatch then 401'd ~91% of the time. Kick infra is institutionalizing raw 100 fallback (docker hard-landing) for this reason — 7 cycles of degraded operation.
background_b1_residue.root_cause_class: trust-boundary-mismatch — picker trusts file-system state, but auth liveness is an Anthropic-side property; only an actual round-trip can prove it.
background_b1_residue.fix_class: live-API health gate with TTL cache (raw 65 idempotent within 30s)

insertions_summary: doc block: top-of-file LIVE-API HEALTH GATE narrative + opt-out env vars
doc block: PROBE SOURCE table now lists 'live_api_probe' as distinct verdict source
fn _probe_cmd_template (default `claude -p ping --model claude-haiku-4-5`; override via CLAUDE_SLOT_PICK_PROBE_CMD for selftest)
fn _pick_with_live_gate (LRU walk with per-candidate live probe + tried-set)
main: --pick now calls _pick_with_live_gate; on full exhaustion emits raw 66 reason=all-slots-unhealthy-live-probe + exit 76
main: --invalidate-cache mode added; emits PASS sentinel reason=live-probe-cache-invalidated
usage banner + sentinel banner + @exit_codes header updated
patch.diff_summary: +~280 net lines; no existing fn signatures changed; backward compatible (CLAUDE_SLOT_PICK_LIVE_PROBE=0 reverts to legacy gate).
patch.chflags_status: FILE WAS NOT LOCKED — `stat -f %Sf` returned `-` (no flags). Edit applied directly; no raw 85 procedure required.
patch.compile_smoke: hexa run claude_slot_pick.hexa executed end-to-end during selftest (4/4 cases) without parser errors → patch is syntactically valid hexa-lang.

selftest.harness_strategy: CLAUDE_SLOT_TEST_HOME redirects slot discovery to a tempdir with 3 mock slots; CLAUDE_SLOT_PICK_PROBE_CMD injects a deterministic shell script in place of `claude -p`. No real Anthropic traffic.

cases.expect: exit 0, stdout = path to .claude-claude2, stderr 'PASS slot=2 reason=lru-available-live-gated'

selftest.execution_log: All 12 assertions PASS in single run on Mac (Darwin 25.4.0, /usr/bin/timeout absent, perl SIGALRM fallback exercised on probe wrap). Wall-clock ≈ 3-4s for full suite.

outcome_detail: Live-API health gate landed in hive/tool/claude_slot_pick.hexa with full selftest (4 cases / 12 assertions PASS). --invalidate-cache flag added. Default behavior is now: every --pick attempts a real claude round-trip (haiku-4-5, 5s timeout) per candidate before returning, walks LRU order to find a healthy slot, or emits raw 66 trailer with actionable fix='run claude /login' on full exhaustion. Cache is 30s TTL at /tmp/claude_slot_health/<slot>.cache. Backward compatible — set CLAUDE_SLOT_PICK_LIVE_PROBE=0 to revert.

F_SPLP.predicate: live-probe latency exceeds the 5s timeout in production (real-world Anthropic round-trip > timeout window) and forces the picker to misclassify a healthy slot as unhealthy
F_SPLP.verifier_command: tail -F ~/.hx/state/markers/claude_slot_pick_*.marker | grep -c 'reason=timeout' over next 24h. Cross-reference with claude.ai service incidents page.
F_SPLP.interpretation_if_violated: Timeout too aggressive for current Anthropic latency profile; raise CLAUDE_SLOT_PICK_PROBE_TIMEOUT to 10s and re-witness. Note: on macOS without coreutils, fallback path uses perl SIGALRM which has been observed to be slightly less precise than GNU `timeout` — investigate per-call wall-clock if F-SPLP-1 fires.

F_SPLP.predicate: false-healthy: the gate returns verdict=healthy for a slot that subsequently 401s on real dispatch within the same 30s cache window (the dispatch happens after slot was probed but slot was revoked between the two)
F_SPLP.verifier_command: for kick events in next 24h: count cases where __SLOT_PICK_RESULT__ PASS slot=N is followed within 30s by __KICK_RESULT__ FAIL reason=*401*. Each match = one F-SPLP-2 hit.
F_SPLP.interpretation_if_violated: 30s cache window is too long given Anthropic-side revocation cadence. Mitigation hierarchy: (a) shorten TTL via CLAUDE_SLOT_PICK_CACHE_TTL=5; (b) force --invalidate-cache after every kick failure; (c) fall back to per-call probe with cache disabled (CLAUDE_SLOT_PICK_CACHE_TTL=0).

F_SPLP.predicate: cache stale-pick: the cached verdict outlives its actual validity — i.e. cache says 'healthy' for slot N at second 0; the user runs `claude /login` for a different slot N at second 25, accidentally invalidating shared OAuth state; at second 28 a pick reuses cached 'healthy' for N but dispatch 401s
F_SPLP.verifier_command: look for any kick FAIL where the immediately preceding __SLOT_LIVE_PROBE__ for the picked slot reads 'source=cache' (not 'source=fresh') and the kick fails reason=*401*. Track in /Users/ghost/.hx/state/markers/claude_slot_pick_*.marker over 24h.
F_SPLP.interpretation_if_violated: OAuth state has cross-slot coupling we did not anticipate. Mitigation: post-`claude /login` flow MUST call `claude_slot_pick --invalidate-cache` (document this in the recovery runbook). If the failure persists, drop cache TTL to 5s or eliminate cache entirely.

raw_91_c3_disclosure: HONEST PARTIAL: (1) Selftest used a MOCKED probe command, not real `claude -p`. The mock proves the gate's plumbing (cache, classification, exit codes, sentinels) is correct; it does NOT prove that real `claude -p ping --model claude-haiku-4-5` actually exits 0 with non-empty stdout when OAuth is healthy, nor that it exits non-zero with '401' string when revoked. The user/operator needs to run the gate against the real B.1 12-profile pool to confirm. Best-effort confidence transfer: B.1 witness already empirically established that `claude -p` does refresh + return exit 0 for claude3 (the 1 healthy profile) and exit non-zero for the other 11; this gate just plumbs that observation into the picker. (2) The 5s timeout is a guess — picked because it covers the ~1.5s observed claude3 refresh from B.1 plus headroom, but real network jitter may push past it. F-SPLP-1 falsifier is preregistered to catch this. (3) `claude -p ping --model claude-haiku-4-5` consumes ~1 token of haiku quota per probe per slot per 30s — at 12 slots * 120 probes/hour this is ~1440 tokens/hour worst-case, ~$0.001/hour at haiku pricing. Negligible but not zero. (4) The fallback path `perl -e 'alarm()' exec()` for macOS without coreutils has not been live-tested with a process that ignores SIGALRM; theoretical edge case where a hung claude CLI ignoring signals would not actually time out. Mitigation: install GNU coreutils (`brew install coreutils`) on every host; the gate prefers `timeout` then `gtimeout` then perl. (5) The patch is syntactically valid hexa (the selftest exercised the full file via `hexa run`), but I have NOT run hexa-lang's own type-checker / linter against it; if hexa has a separate `--typecheck` mode, that should be invoked before merging. (6) chflags status check: file was NOT locked (raw 85 not needed); but if a hexa builder process re-locks it during the next CI cycle, future edits would need raw 85 procedure (sudo chflags nouchg).

korean_report_300char: 1) 패치: ~/core/hive/tool/claude_slot_pick.hexa (39275→47350B). _live_api_probe / _pick_with_live_gate / --invalidate-cache 추가; --pick 가 5s timeout 으로 `claude -p ping --model claude-haiku-4-5` round-trip 후 healthy 만 반환, 12개 모두 unhealthy 면 raw 66 trailer + exit 76. 2) Selftest 4/4 PASS (12 assertions): C1 all-401→exit76, C2 1-healthy→pick claude2, C3 cache-hit, C4 invalidate→re-probe. 3) Cache 정책: 30s TTL @ /tmp/claude_slot_health/<N>.cache, atomic_write, --invalidate-cache 명시 무효화 검증. 4) raw 91 C3: 셀프테스트는 mock probe — 실제 12-profile pool 검증은 사용자가 production 에서 1회 수행 필요; 5s timeout 은 B.1 의 1.5s claude3 refresh 관측치 + 헤드룸 추정치 (F-SPLP-1 falsifier 가 모니터). 5) chflags: 잠겨있지 않음 (raw 85 절차 불필요).
